items.title: Trump renews Greenland threats at NATO summit, says U.S. could remove troops from Europe
items.summary: NATO fell into a crisis earlier this year as Trump demanded that the U.S. must take control of Greenland on national security grounds.

items.title: Chinese lidar maker with Nvidia ties accused of being cyber risk for U.S.
items.summary: Hesai Technology was blacklisted as a national security threat in 2024 by the U.S. Department of Defense, which designated Hesai as a Chinese military entity.

items.title: Stellantis to sell small Fiat Topolino EV for $13,995 in U.S.
items.summary: While the Topolino resembles a small car such as the Fiat 500, the EV is actually a quadricycle that functions more like a golf cart.

items.title: Rivian stock falls more than 10% as company sells 75 million shares to raise capital
items.summary: The capital raise occurred during extended hours trading following Rivian shares increasing 8.1% on Monday after increasing 19.2% last week.

items.title: Trump ally Nigel Farage quits UK parliament amid finance scandal to fight special election
items.summary: Reform U.K. leader Nigel Farage has quit as MP for Clacton, forcing a by-election that he has said he will fight.

items.title: Buy these quality, low-stress stocks for the summer, says Jefferies
items.summary: This summer, Jefferies recommends focusing on some high-quality stocks that have been left behind by the AI momentum trade.

items.title: Amazon raising at least $25 billion in bond sale, won't issue more debt in 2026
items.summary: It marks Amazon's latest debt raise as it looks to buttress its massive investments in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Toyota to invest $3.6 billion to move Tacoma pickup truck production from Mexico to Texas
items.summary: Toyota said it is investing $3.6 billion to move production of the Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio manufacturing campus in Texas.

items.title: Trump arrives in Turkey as NATO is strained by Russian attacks, U.S. impatience
items.summary: Trump frequently vented about NATO members' refusal to heed U.S. calls for help clearing the Strait of Hormuz during its campaign against Iran.

items.title: Billionaires John and Laura Arnold commit $2.6 million to study online sports betting risk
items.summary: John Arnold and Laura Arnold, who run Arnold Ventures, are investing $2.6 million for university research into potential harms of online sports betting.

items.title: Far more real estate agents now report seeing a balanced market, CNBC Housing Market Survey finds
items.summary: Agents who reported at least one price cut to active listings dropped dramatically from prior surveys.

items.title: Graham Platner denies sex assault claim as Democrats urge him to quit Maine Senate race
items.summary: Graham Platner hopes to unseat Republican Sen. Susan Collins, but the Democrat's campaign is reeling from a bombshell allegation by Jenny Racicot.

items.title: We're trimming a rotation winner and adding to an exciting new stock
items.summary: A late-day reversal in one of our newest stocks gives us an opportunity to buy more shares.

items.title: Trump Accounts are live. Here's how some families plan to use them
items.summary: As Trump Accounts launch, millions of parents are figuring out how the new investment accounts fit with their family's financial goals.

items.title: World Cup in photos: Belgium beats U.S. 4-1 after Balogun controversy, Ronaldo exits
items.summary: The U.S. is out of the World Cup, losing 4-1 to Belgium in the Round of 16 in a match blemished by suspension of Folarin Balogun's red card. Ronaldo exits.

items.title: Hanwha Ocean shares sink 23% as it loses bid to build Canada's next fleet of submarines
items.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Monday that Germany's Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems would be the preferred supplier for delivering the submarines.

items.title: Google backs nuclear fusion startup targeting Europe’s first commercial power plant
items.summary: Proxima Fusion has raised $468 million as it looks to move towards commercializing the promising but infamously difficult technical challenge of nuclear fusion.

items.title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation
items.summary: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba has put Anthropic's Claude Code on a high-risk software list.

items.title: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with U.S. companies as OpenAI, Anthropic costs surge
items.summary: Recent model releases from Chinese companies including DeepSeek and Z.ai are seen by many as highly competitive compared to leading U.S. frontier systems.

items.title: Why oil investors fear the next toll fight could be the Strait of Malacca
items.summary: The prospect of fees to transit the Strait of Hormuz has sparked alarm, not least by investors who fear it could be replicated in other maritime corridors.

items.title: Student loan servicers begin 90-day countdown for borrowers to leave SAVE plan
items.summary: Student loan servicers have begun alerting borrowers that they have 90 days to leave the Biden-era SAVE plan. Here's what comes next.

items.title: India fashion retailer Trent tumbles 11% as revenue growth disappoints
items.summary: Shares of one of India's biggest fashion retailers, Trent, fell over 10% after the company reported weak earnings growth.

items.title: Klarna seeks U.S. bank charter in latest push beyond buy now, pay later
items.summary: Klarna joins a wave of fintech and crypto firms seeking entry to the traditional banking system.

items.title: To future-proof your career in the age of AI, do 3 simple things: You'll 'remain valuable,' says expert
items.summary: Dan Schawbel, who's spent over a decade researching how technology reshapes careers, says employees who do these 3 things in the age of AI will "come out ahead."

items.title: Here's how SpaceX's Nasdaq-100 inclusion might affect options pricing
items.summary: About half-a-million SpaceX options traded by midday Monday, a little below the average since inception.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, as Xbox unit downsizes and plans to spin off four gaming studios
items.summary: Microsoft is cutting jobs in its commercial business and its Xbox gaming group, where revenue has been shrinking.

items.title: Rivian, Tesla and the 'Holly Index': How to trade the new EV Main Street battle
items.summary: Mike Khouw gives a trade on Rivian and Tesla, informed by his wife's recent auto purchase.

items.title: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell to donate stock to Trump Accounts
items.summary: President Donald Trump told CNBC's Joe Kernen last week that he thought SpaceX CEO Elon Musk would donate company stock to the program.

items.title: Record heat, crowds drive offseason boom in international travel
items.summary: Airlines are extending flight schedules to maximize on lucrative international travel.

items.title: Lockheed Martin to buy naval defense firm Ultra Maritime for $3.45 billion
items.summary: Ultra is owned by private equity firm Advent International, and specialises in anti-submarine technology.
